claims.claim: `ViscosityVector` names density, elasticity, cohesion, persistence, residual ghost weight, flow rate, and effective mobility as review context rather than live control.
claims.rationale: Source review confirmed the explicit advisory boundary and the vector fields used to make stuckness, ghost weight, and mobility legible.

claims.claim: With fixed flow rate, rising persistence should reduce effective mobility, and high residual ghost weight should remain visible as heavier lingering texture.
claims.rationale: Existing regulator tests verify rigid versus yielding effective mobility and residual ghost weight tracking without converting the readout into active pressure or damping authority.

claims.claim: Transition viscosity from passive readout to an active input for PI control logic by scaling gate opening or flow rate with a viscosity compensation factor.
claims.rationale: This would change live regulator/controller behavior. Existing code already exposes inert comfort-gate and damping previews; making them active needs explicit Mike/operator approval and post-change health/fill monitoring.
